security_invariants: Vendor-hosted agents receive a short-lived scoped MCP token reference, never a raw token in the dev brief, wake, log, or activity stream.
The canonical repository and non-default task branch are fixed by Switchboard; a provider may not redirect work to main or master.
Provider authentication and GitHub repository grants are checked before launch.
A successful provider request is only queued; running requires a read-back provider session id and app-visible session URL.
A lost, expired, or unreadable provider session becomes a visible failed state and never falls back to a self-hosted process.
Switchboard applies a lower per-vendor concurrency cap even when the provider permits more sessions.
PR/default-branch provenance remains the code-truth completion authority; vendor completion is not task Done.
Subscription spend remains reported or unknown until provider reconciliation and is never fabricated as exact per-task cost.

billing.notes: Record tokens when exposed, but do not invent per-task subscription cost.

vendors.unsupported_reason: Official Codex cloud docs describe web, GitHub, Linear, and Slack initiation but do not document a public API that creates a Codex cloud task and returns a bindable task id plus URL. Codex SDK/App Server execute Codex in caller-controlled compute and must not be mislabeled vendor-hosted cloud execution.

billing.notes: Capture usage only when an official task receipt or provider reconciliation exposes it.

session.continuity: fresh_agent; same-agent follow-up run supported; exact same-run resume unsupported

billing.notes: Report provider-exposed tokens/cost; reconcile later if a billing export is available.
